calendar.matters:
ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-24-2023 Off Calendar

Motion To Dismiss For Three Year Delay In Service Of Summons; And To Quash Service Of Summons
Off Calendar Jul-14-2023 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

RE: Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to dismiss and quash service of summons; request for judicial notice.

Appearances:

Liana Ter-Oganesyan, Esq. and Karin Mayelan, Esq., via Zoom, K & L Associates, (424) 205-1860, for Plaintiff.

Zuzana S. Ikels, Esq., in court, Deputy Attorney, City and County of San Francisco, (415) 355-3307, for Defendant.

Ruling - ARGUED. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions regarding the subject motion. The court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts its tentative ruling as follows: Defendant City and County of San Francisco's motion to dismiss and quash service of summons is granted. Defendant's request for judicial notice is granted. This case was filed on May-6-2020. The court dismissed the case on July 20, 2022, due to Plaintiff's failure to comply with court orders and rules. See Ikels Decl., Ex. B. After the dismissal, Plaintiff served Defendant on Jul-22-2022 by purported substituted service. The service was both improper (as not served on the correct person) and void because case had been dismissed. As noted in Graf v. Gaslight, (1990) 225 Cal.App.3d 291, 297, it is legally impossible for a plaintiff to serve a summons after a dismissal. Plaintiff does not address this argument. Furthermore, service has not been accomplished within the three-year period of CCP section 583.210, requiring dismissal of the case.

Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare and submit a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling.

Judge: Curtis E. A. Karnow. Clerk: Robert Woods. Court Reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR: 13714, T: (415) 601.7054, E: apourtabib@gmail.com. (302/CK)

RULING - The matter is Uncontested. The Court adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

PLAINTIFF SILVA AVOYAN's Motion To Set Aside/Vacate Dismissal.

Plaintiff's unopposed "motion to set aside/vacate dismissal" is granted.

Order is signed.

Judge: Richard B. Ulmer Jr., Clerk: M. Goodman, Not Reported. (302/rbu)

ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jul-20-2022 Dismissal
MINI MINUTES: Kenneth Tsang, Esq. specially appearing for the plaintiff. Plaintiff requests that the order to show cause be continued to allow time to serve defendant CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO. Plaintiff's request is denied. The Court orders the case dismissed without prejudice. The court finds no good cause to allow the complaint to keep going. JUDGE: ANNE-CHRISTINE MASSULLO; Clerk: GGonzales; Proceedings not reported.
